dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 1355 RH: Weatherization Enhancement and Readiness Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Tonko (for himself, Ms. Kaptur, Mr. Riley of New York, Mr. Moylan, and Mr. Lawler) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

Additional sponsors: Mr. Mullin, Mr. Fitzpatrick, Mr. Nunn of Iowa, Mr. Bishop, Ms. Castor of Florida, Ms. Goodlander, Mr. Correa, Mr. Mannion, Ms. McClellan, Mr. Magaziner, Ms. Titus, Mr. Moskowitz, Mr. Moulton, Mr. Olszewski, Mr. Khanna, Mr. Min, and Mr. Pappas
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on February 13, 2025

form.official-title: To amend the Energy Conservation and Production Act to reauthorize the Weatherization Assistance Program, direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a weatherization readiness program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Weatherization Enhancement and Readiness Act of 2025.

subsection.text: Section 415(c)(1) of the Energy Conservation and Production Act (42 U.S.C. 6865(c)(1)) is amended by striking $6,500 and inserting $12,000.
Section 422 of the Energy Conservation and Production Act (42 U.S.C. 6872) is amended by striking paragraphs (1) and (2) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: $300,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2026 through 2028;

section.text: Section 414D(i) of the Energy Conservation and Production Act (42 U.S.C. 6864d(i)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), by striking ; and and inserting a semicolon;
in paragraph (2), by striking achieved. and inserting achieved; and; and
by adding at the end the following:
the impacts of enhancement and innovation readiness efforts on eligibility for the weatherization program conducted under this part.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
